title: SUMX(FILTER()) anti-pattern
description: SUMX(FILTER(...)) forces row-by-row Formula Engine evaluation, preventing query fusion and parallelization. 5-10x slower than CALCULATE.
fix_suggestion: Replace with CALCULATE(AGG(Column), filter_condition)

title: COUNTROWS(FILTER()) anti-pattern
description: COUNTROWS(FILTER(...)) materializes the entire filtered table in memory. Use CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(Table), condition) instead.
fix_suggestion: Replace with CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(Table), filter_condition)

title: FORMAT() forces Formula Engine evaluation
description: FORMAT() is not SE-pushable and forces the entire expression into the Formula Engine, which is single-threaded. Prefer format strings on measures or client-side formatting.
fix_suggestion: Use measure FormatString property or conditional formatting instead of FORMAT()

title: ADDCOLUMNS(SUMMARIZE()) — use SUMMARIZECOLUMNS instead
description: ADDCOLUMNS wrapping SUMMARIZE creates an intermediate table then adds columns. SUMMARIZECOLUMNS is a single optimized operation that handles both grouping and aggregation.
fix_suggestion: Replace with SUMMARIZECOLUMNS(group_cols, measure_name, expression)

title: FILTER(ALL/ALLSELECTED()) — prefer CALCULATE with filter
description: Using FILTER over ALL or ALLSELECTED materializes the full unfiltered table before applying the predicate. CALCULATE with direct conditions lets the engine optimize the filter.
fix_suggestion: Move the predicate into CALCULATE as a filter argument

title: FILTER(ALLSELECTED()) — prefer CALCULATE with filter
description: Using FILTER over ALLSELECTED materializes the full selection-filtered table before applying the predicate. CALCULATE with direct conditions is more efficient.
fix_suggestion: Move the predicate into CALCULATE as a filter argument

title: SUMX over single column — use SUM instead
description: SUMX iterating a table with a simple column reference as expression is equivalent to SUM(column) but prevents SE optimization. SUM is always preferred for single-column aggregation.
fix_suggestion: Replace SUMX(Table, Table[Column]) with SUM(Table[Column])

title: Deeply nested CALCULATE (>2 levels)
description: More than 2 levels of nested CALCULATE creates complex filter context transitions that are hard to optimize and reason about. Each level adds context-transition overhead.
fix_suggestion: Flatten nested CALCULATE by combining filter conditions or extracting into VARs

title: COUNTROWS(VALUES()) — use DISTINCTCOUNT instead
description: COUNTROWS(VALUES(column)) materializes the distinct values table. DISTINCTCOUNT(column) is a single optimized operation.
fix_suggestion: Replace with DISTINCTCOUNT(column)

title: RELATED inside iterator — consider denormalization or CALCULATE
description: RELATED inside SUMX or other iterators traverses relationships row-by-row. For large tables, consider using CALCULATE or denormalizing the column.
fix_suggestion: Consider CALCULATE with RELATEDTABLE or denormalize the column into the fact table

title: CROSSFILTER in measure — model relationship change preferred
description: CROSSFILTER changes relationship behavior at query time. Prefer configuring the relationship in the model for consistent and optimized filtering.
fix_suggestion: Configure cross-filter direction in the model relationship instead of using CROSSFILTER in DAX

title: USERELATIONSHIP in measure — consider active relationship
description: USERELATIONSHIP activates inactive relationships at query time. Consider whether the relationship should be active by default to avoid per-query overhead.
fix_suggestion: If this relationship is used frequently, consider making it the active relationship in the model

title: LOOKUPVALUE — prefer RELATED with physical relationship
description: LOOKUPVALUE performs a linear scan. If a relationship exists between the tables, RELATED uses the indexed relationship path and is significantly faster.
fix_suggestion: Create a relationship between the tables and use RELATED(column) instead

title: EARLIER/EARLIEST is deprecated — use VAR
description: EARLIER and EARLIEST are deprecated functions that navigate row contexts by ordinal position. Using VAR to capture the current row value is clearer and more efficient.
fix_suggestion: Capture the value with VAR _currentValue = [Column] before the inner iterator, then reference _currentValue

title: IF(ISBLANK()) forces dense evaluation
description: IF(ISBLANK([x]), 0, [x]) converts BLANK to 0, forcing dense evaluation across all rows including those that would naturally be BLANK. This increases memory and processing time.
fix_suggestion: Allow BLANK propagation where possible, or use COALESCE([x], 0) only when truly needed

title: COUNTROWS(DISTINCT()) — use DISTINCTCOUNT instead
description: COUNTROWS(DISTINCT(column)) materializes the distinct values table. DISTINCTCOUNT(column) is a single optimized operation.
fix_suggestion: Replace with DISTINCTCOUNT(column)

title: VALUES in scalar context — prefer SELECTEDVALUE
description: When VALUES is used to get a single value (e.g., in IF or variable assignment), SELECTEDVALUE is more explicit and handles multi-value gracefully with an alternate result.
fix_suggestion: Use SELECTEDVALUE(column, alternateResult) for clearer intent

title: CALCULATETABLE(FILTER()) — redundant wrapping
description: CALCULATETABLE already applies filters; wrapping FILTER inside it is redundant. CALCULATETABLE directly supports Boolean filter conditions.
fix_suggestion: Replace with CALCULATETABLE(Table, filter_condition) without FILTER wrapper

title: NATURALLEFTOUTERJOIN forces FE evaluation
description: NATURALLEFTOUTERJOIN is fully evaluated in the Formula Engine and cannot be pushed to the Storage Engine. Consider using SUMMARIZECOLUMNS or model relationships.
fix_suggestion: Use model relationships with SUMMARIZECOLUMNS or CALCULATE where possible

title: GENERATE forces nested iteration in FE
description: GENERATE evaluates the second table expression for every row of the first, entirely in the Formula Engine. For large tables, this is extremely slow.
fix_suggestion: Use SUMMARIZECOLUMNS or model relationships to achieve the same result with SE optimization
